response: 263 is prime. It's not divisible by any prime less than or equal to its square root (≈16.2), specifically 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, or 13.
reasoning_content: We need to check if 263 is prime. A quick sanity check: check divisibility by small primes: 2 (no, odd), 3 (sum digits 2+6+3=11, not divisible by 3), 5 (doesn't end with 0 or 5), 7? 7*37=259, 7*38=266, so no. 11? 11*23=253, 11*24=264, so no. 13? 13*20=260, 13*21=273, so no. 17? 17*15=255, 17*16=272, no. 19? 19*13=247, 19*14=266, no. 23? 23*11=253, 23*12=276, no. 29? 29*9=261, 29*10=290, no. 31? 31*8=248, 31*9=279, no. 37? 37*7=259, 37*8=296, no. 41? 41*6=246, 41*7=287, no. 43? 43*6=258, 43*7=301, no. Next prime sqrt(263) ~ 16.2, actually sqrt(263) is about 16.2, so we only need to check primes up to 13 (since 17^2=289>263). We checked up to 13, none divide. So 263 is prime. Quick sanity: yes.
